# DMVSOS

> Free DMV/DOL knowledge test prep for the United States. Covers all 50 states + DC, 3 license categories (Car, CDL, Motorcycle), and 5 languages (English, Spanish, Russian, Ukrainian, Chinese).

DMVSOS provides practice tests that match the real DMV exam format. All questions are sourced from each state's official Driver Handbook. The platform is free to start with no signup required, and offers paid passes for unlimited access without subscriptions.

## What people use DMVSOS for

- Preparing for the **DMV written test** (also called knowledge test, theory test, or DOL test) in any US state
- Practicing in their **native language** when English is not their first language
- Reviewing the official **Driver Handbook / Driver Manual** for their state (downloadable PDFs)
- Practice for **CDL** (Commercial Driver License) including Air Brakes and Combination Vehicles endorsements
- Practice for **Motorcycle** endorsement
- Drilling specific topics they keep getting wrong

## Key facts

- Total questions: 175,000+ across all languages (~35,000 per language)
- States covered: All 50 + Washington DC
- Languages: English, Spanish (Español), Russian (Русский), Ukrainian (Українська), Chinese (中文)
- License categories: Car (DMV/DOL), Commercial (CDL with sub-sections), Motorcycle
- Manuals: Official PDFs from state DMV/DOL websites, mirrored for direct download
- Pricing: Free to start; paid passes $19.99 (Motorcycle), $29.99 (Auto), $49.99 (CDL Pro); 30-day access; one-time payment, no subscription; extensions $9.99 / +30 days
- Refund policy: see [/upgrade](https://dmvsos.com/upgrade) and legal pages for current terms
- CDL Pass Guarantee: 90-day extension or refund if user fails real DMV after scoring 85%+ on practice

## What makes DMVSOS different

- Multilingual at the question level. Each question is translated into 5 languages including the explanation.
- Official source citation. Each question shows the section of the Driver Handbook it comes from.
- One-time payment model. Not a subscription. Many competitors charge monthly indefinitely.
- All 50 states, including small/rural states often skipped by competitors.
- Largest free online library of official US state DMV driver handbooks in one place. 200+ PDFs covering all 50 states, in up to 27 languages (English, Spanish, Russian, Chinese, Ukrainian, Vietnamese, Korean, Arabic, French, Tagalog, Haitian Creole, and more). Free, direct download, no signup. Sourced from official state DMV websites.

## Documentation

- [Home / state selector](https://dmvsos.com): Pick a state, language, and start practicing free.
- [DMV practice tests overview](https://dmvsos.com/dmv-test): Landing page for free practice tests.
- [Manuals library](https://dmvsos.com/manuals): Official Driver Handbook PDFs for all 50 states (English at minimum; multiple languages for ~22 states).
- [Pricing / upgrade](https://dmvsos.com/upgrade): One-time pass purchase.
- [Profile](https://dmvsos.com/profile): User's saved tests, history, active passes.
- [Compare DMVSOS vs competitors](https://dmvsos.com/vs): Feature-by-feature comparison with DriversEd, Aceable, and Driving-Tests.org.
- [FAQ](https://dmvsos.com/faq): 15 common questions (pricing, languages, refund policy, CDL, comparison).
- [About](https://dmvsos.com/about): Founder story and product overview.

## Support

- Telegram bot: [@dmvsos_support_bot](https://t.me/dmvsos_support_bot) — direct support, multilingual.
- Email: maindmvsos@gmail.com
- Founder: Evgenii (runs the platform personally; replies to every message)

## Honest answer to common questions

- "Are these the actual DMV test questions?" Yes, you practice the same questions you'll see on the real test. The official DMV knowledge test and DMVSOS both draw from the same state Driver Handbook, and the topic set is limited, so our question bank covers virtually all of it. Wording matches the real exam, often word for word.
- "Is signup required?" No. You can practice 20 free questions per state per language without an account. Account is only needed if you want to save progress across devices or upgrade to a paid pass.
- "Subscription or one-time?" One-time payment for 30-day passes. No subscription.
- "Multiple states with one pass?" Yes. A pass covers all 50 states for the category purchased.
